legalName: PT Coco Reina Global Charcoal Indonesia
tagline: Coconut shell charcoal briquettes, factory-direct from Semarang

address.street: Kawasan Industri Candi, Jl. Gatot Subroto Blk. 3B No.18

whatsapp.defaultMessage: Hello, I am interested in your coconut charcoal briquettes.

presetMessages.heroCta: Hello PT Coco Reina — I would like to request a wholesale quote for coconut shisha charcoal briquettes. Could you share your price list and MOQ details?
presetMessages.salesGeneral: Hello, I am a wholesale buyer interested in your coconut shisha charcoal briquettes. Please share specifications, pricing, and lead time.
presetMessages.directorIntro: Hello Mr. Sinno — I would like to discuss a wholesale order of coconut shisha charcoal directly with you.
presetMessages.videoCallRequest: Hello, I would like to schedule a WhatsApp video call to see your factory and discuss a wholesale order. What times work for you in GMT+7?
presetMessages.piRequest: Hello, I would like to request a Proforma Invoice for coconut shisha charcoal briquettes. Please share your bank details and payment terms after KYC.
presetMessages.sampleRequest: Hello Mulia Charcoal — I would like to request free samples of your coconut shisha charcoal shipped to my address. Please confirm the courier cost to my country.
presetMessages.samplesHero: Hello Mulia Charcoal — please send free samples of your coconut shisha charcoal so I can verify the burn specs in our lab before placing a wholesale order.
presetMessages.samplesProducts: Hello Mulia Charcoal — I would like free samples of the {{product}} coconut shisha charcoal to evaluate before placing a wholesale order.
presetMessages.quoteSpecs: Hello Mulia Charcoal — please send your latest USD price list FOB Semarang for the Premium, Super Premium, and Platinum grades, including MOQ and lead time.
presetMessages.factoryAbout: Hello Mulia Charcoal — I would like more information about your factory: capacity, certifications, and a virtual tour. Could you share documents and a video call slot?
presetMessages.oemLayout: Hello Mulia Charcoal — please share your OEM packaging layouts and master-box / inner-box dimensions so I can plan our private-label order.
presetMessages.oemDesigner: Hello Mulia Charcoal — I would like to discuss a private-label / OEM brand with your designer. We have artwork and target SKUs ready to share.
presetMessages.contactDirect: Hello Mulia Charcoal — I am evaluating a wholesale order from {{country}}. Could we start a direct conversation about specs, price, and shipping?

commercial.paymentTerms: 50% T/T advance, 50% T/T 3 days before loading

transitTimes.destination: New York / New Jersey, USA (East Coast)

production.storageNote: Store sealed in a cool, dry place away from direct sunlight and humidity. Re-sealable inner packaging preserves the burn profile over the full shelf life.

processSteps.title: Carbonization
processSteps.note: Raw coconut shells are carbonized in our kilns, driving off volatiles and leaving high-fixed-carbon charcoal.

processSteps.title: Milling & sieving
processSteps.note: Carbonized shell is crushed and milled to a fine powder, then sieved to a controlled, uniform particle size.

processSteps.title: Mixing & binder
processSteps.note: Charcoal powder is blended with food-grade tapioca starch and water — no chemical accelerants — into a homogeneous paste.

processSteps.title: Pressing & forming
processSteps.note: The paste is pressed and extruded into cubes, hexagonals, or fingers at high pressure for a dense, consistent briquette.

processSteps.title: Drying
processSteps.note: Formed briquettes are dried in temperature-controlled tunnels to a low, stable moisture content.

processSteps.title: Weathering
processSteps.note: Briquettes are weathered under cover before packing — a dangerous-goods safety step that also stabilizes the burn.

processSteps.title: Quality control & packing
processSteps.note: Every batch passes our in-process quality checks and a burn/ash test before packing; a reference sample is retained.

qcSteps.title: Raw shell intake
qcSteps.checks: Incoming coconut shell is inspected for moisture and contamination before carbonization.

qcSteps.title: Carbonization control
qcSteps.checks: Kiln temperature and burn time are monitored to hold fixed carbon within target.

qcSteps.title: Powder consistency
qcSteps.checks: Milled charcoal is sieved and checked for particle-size uniformity.

qcSteps.title: Mix & density check
qcSteps.checks: Binder ratio and paste density are verified before pressing.

qcSteps.title: In-line dimensional check
qcSteps.checks: Pressed briquettes are sampled for size, weight, and edge integrity.

qcSteps.title: Finished-batch burn test
qcSteps.checks: Each batch undergoes a moisture reading and a burn/ash test; a reference sample is retained.

equipment.name: Coconut shell crushers
equipment.spec: primary size reduction of carbonized shell

equipment.name: Hammer mills
equipment.spec: fine milling to a uniform charcoal powder

equipment.name: Temperature-controlled drying tunnels
equipment.spec: moisture reduction to a low, stable level

factory.sourcingAshNarrative: Coconut shells from {{sourcingRegion}} are mature and thick-walled, which typically supports a denser charcoal with low, light-grey ash. Exact ash content is confirmed per order, not guaranteed by region alone.
factory.ownerNote: We built this factory in Semarang to do one thing exceptionally well — coconut shisha charcoal. I'm on the floor with my team every week, because consistency is something you supervise, not something you outsource.
factory.laborStatement: Our workforce is employed on formal terms, with wages at or above the regional minimum and a factory run to local occupational health-and-safety standards. We use no child labor, and our coconut-shell sourcing supports smallholder farming communities across eastern Indonesia.
factory.ramadanLeadNote: Demand peaks ahead of Ramadan and the summer shisha season. To hold your delivery window through these surges, place confirmed orders 6–8 weeks earlier than usual; we schedule production slots on a first-confirmed basis.

samples.sampleNote: Lab-grade 1 kg samples include a batch Certificate of Analysis (COA) so you can verify the burn specifications independently before the wholesale order.

terms: T/T 50/50 (50% deposit, 50% balance 3 days before loading)

bank.accountName: PT Coco Reina Global Charcoal Indonesia
bank.bankAddress: Jl. MH Thamrin No. 1, Jakarta Pusat 10310

masterBox.notes: Single- or double-wall corrugated; printed or kraft.

innerBox.notes: Full-colour CMYK printing on 230 gsm board; matte or gloss finish.

primaryPlastic.notes: Printed inner plastic film; your logo + lot code per buyer requirements.

dg.sp979scope: SP 979 applies to UN 1362 (CARBON, ACTIVATED) only — not to non-activated coconut shell charcoal.
dg.rationale: Carriers link 68 container-vessel fires to charcoal between 2015 and 2022, with ignition often occurring days after loading.
dg.carrierEnforcementNote: Several lines enforced SP 978 early; some have since declined charcoal bookings altogether. Confirm current acceptance at booking.

sht.note: A Self-Heating Test (UN Test N.4) report is available on request as supporting hazard evidence; since IMDG Amendment 42-24 it no longer exempts UN 1361 from dangerous-goods carriage.

sp978.weatheringMethod: 14 days under cover in open air, or inert gas after pyrolysis plus 24 hours of storage
dgdFields: temperature on the day of packing
sp978.n4Note: Since IMDG Amendment 42-24, a passing UN N.4 self-heating test no longer exempts UN 1361 charcoal from dangerous-goods carriage — it is supporting evidence only.
sp978.stowageNote: Stack height no more than 1.5 m, or a maximum block of 16 m³ with at least 15 cm between blocks.

documentsStandard.name: Commercial Invoice
documentsStandard.issuer: PT Coco Reina Global Charcoal Indonesia

documentsStandard.name: Packing List
documentsStandard.issuer: PT Coco Reina Global Charcoal Indonesia
documentsStandard.buyerUse: Customs and warehouse reconciliation against the container

documentsStandard.name: Bill of Lading (B/L)
documentsStandard.buyerUse: Title document the consignee needs to claim the container at destination

documentsStandard.name: Certificate of Origin (COO)
documentsStandard.issuer: Ministry of Trade via an appointed IPSKA agency (e-SKA)
documentsStandard.buyerUse: Origin evidence and, where applicable, duty preference at destination

documentsStandard.name: Material Safety Data Sheet (MSDS / SDS)
documentsStandard.issuer: PT Coco Reina Global Charcoal Indonesia
documentsStandard.buyerUse: Hazard evidence requested by the carrier and importer

documentsStandard.name: Dangerous Goods Declaration (DGD)
documentsStandard.issuer: PT Coco Reina Global Charcoal Indonesia
documentsStandard.buyerUse: Declares UN 1361, Class 4.2, Packing Group III and the SP 978 data fields to the carrier

documentsStandard.name: Container Vanning / Stuffing Certificate
documentsStandard.buyerUse: Confirms the container is packed and secured per the IMDG Code

documentsAdditional.name: Self-Heating Test (SHT) report
documentsAdditional.buyerUse: Supporting hazard evidence within the dangerous-goods shipment

documentsAdditional.name: Certificate of Analysis (COA)
documentsAdditional.buyerUse: Independent verification of the burn specifications

documentsAdditional.name: Fumigation / Phytosanitary Certificate
documentsAdditional.issuer: Licensed fumigation provider or quarantine authority
documentsAdditional.buyerUse: Required by some destinations for wood packaging or plant-origin goods
documentsAdditional.providedWhen: On request, where the destination requires it

insurance.basis: Buyer-arranged by default — under EXW / FOB / CFR the factory arranges no cover and the buyer insures the cargo. Marine insurance can be arranged by the factory on request under CIF (seller-arranged cover to the destination port); inherent-vice perils stay excluded under any term.

coo.issuer: Ministry of Trade via the e-SKA system, physically issued by appointed IPSKA agencies
coo.types: Preferential SKA (Form A / D / E — secures a reduced duty under a trade agreement at destination) or non-preferential (Form B — origin evidence only)

surchargeStack.label: Ocean base freight
surchargeStack.note: Quoted by the carrier at booking.

htsCandidates.note: "Of shell or nut" — most precise for coconut shell charcoal; base duty Free.
Residual "Other"; base duty Free. Legacy CBP ruling N306942 (2019) cited 4402.90.0000 before the post-2022 renumbering.

usa.htsNotes: Both candidates carry a Free base (MFN) duty, so the exact 10-digit code does not change the rate; a CBP binding-ruling request would settle it. Confirm classification with your customs broker.

dutyLayers.label: Section 122 balance-of-payments surcharge
dutyLayers.basis: Flat global surcharge (Trade Act 1974, Proclamation 11012), assessed in addition to MFN
dutyLayers.legalStatus: Struck by the Court of International Trade on 7 May 2026 (party-specific; on appeal to the Federal Circuit) — collection continues for non-plaintiff importers; statutory sunset around 24 July 2026. Most sources report 10%; confirm at booking.

dutyLayers.label: Merchandise Processing Fee (MPF)
dutyLayers.basis: Formal entry; minimum $33.58, maximum $651.50 per entry (FY2026)

dutyLayers.label: Harbor Maintenance Fee (HMF)
dutyLayers.basis: Vessel imports only; assessed on cargo value

usa.dutyHistory: The MFN base is Free. The reciprocal layer has moved fast: +32% (IEEPA, EO 14257, April 2025) → about 19% (US–Indonesia agreement, February 2026) → the IEEPA basis was voided by the Supreme Court on 20 February 2026 and collection halted from about 24 February 2026 → replaced by a 10% Section 122 surcharge (effective 24 February 2026), itself struck by the CIT on 7 May 2026 (party-specific, on appeal) and set to sunset around 24 July 2026. The effective rate is roughly 10% ad valorem today, heavily caveated; a Section 301 investigation opened 11 March 2026 may succeed it.
usa.adcvd: No anti-dumping or countervailing-duty order was found for Chapter 44 charcoal from Indonesia in the ITA database; confirm with your customs broker.

entryNotes.isf: The Importer Security Filing (ISF, "10+2") must be transmitted at least 24 hours before the cargo is laden aboard the vessel at the origin port. The importer files ten data elements: seller; buyer; importer-of-record number; consignee number; manufacturer or supplier; ship-to party; country of origin; HTSUS number; container stuffing location; and consolidator.
entryNotes.bond: A continuous or single-entry customs bond is required and must include ISF provisions; ISF penalties run to $5,000 per violation, up to $10,000 per filing.
entryNotes.entrySummary: Entry is finalized on CBP Form 7501; the data is reusable between the ISF and the 7501.

items.label: Deeming Rule
items.value: FDA Deeming Rule, 81 FR 28974 (10 May 2016; effective 8 August 2016).

items.label: Scope
items.value: Hookah charcoal is regulated as a component or part of a tobacco product when it maintains combustion of waterpipe tobacco (81 FR 28974 at 29019), regardless of the material it is made from.

items.label: Requirements
items.value: Establishment registration, product listing, and a premarket tobacco product application (PMTA) before lawful US introduction — hookah charcoal is a new tobacco product, not grandfathered before 15 February 2007.

items.label: Enforcement discretion
items.value: The FDA has not adopted a broad enforcement-discretion policy for hookah charcoal; a pending application is not a safe harbor, the posture is not codified, and it can change without notice.

items.label: Import enforcement
items.value: Import Alert 98-06 (detention without physical examination of new tobacco products without marketing authorization); CBP may detain or refuse entry.

items.label: Do not mislabel
items.value: Labeling the product as BBQ or general-purpose charcoal does not change FDA jurisdiction; misbranded or adulterated tobacco products are subject to detention or refusal, and misdeclaration is separately actionable.

items.label: Practical steps
items.value: Work with US counsel and a customs broker experienced in tobacco-product entries before you ship; confirm registration, listing, and marketing-authorization status for your specific product.

htsCandidates.note: "Of shells or nuts" — the correct, most specific UK commodity code for coconut-shell charcoal briquettes (GRI 3(a)); base MFN duty 0%. Declare this, not 4402.90.

Residual "Other" — wrong for coconut-shell charcoal. Declaring under this code mis-signals a generic timber-derived product and raises the CDS risk-engine likelihood of a documentary (Route 1) or physical (Route 2) check; misdeclaration is actionable even where the duty outcome is identical.

uk.htsNotes: Heading 4402 covers "wood charcoal (including shell or nut charcoal), whether or not agglomerated"; the natural starch binder does not change the classification. Subheading 4402.20 ("of shells or nuts") is more specific than 4402.90 ("other") under GRI 3(a), so 4402 20 00 00 is the defensible code. Both carry a 0% base rate, so the choice does not change the duty — but declaring 4402.20 signals an agricultural byproduct rather than timber and reduces the chance of a customs hold. Confirm the live line on the UK Integrated Online Tariff with your customs broker each shipment.

dutyLayers.label: Customs duty (UKGT MFN / DCTS)
dutyLayers.basis: UK Global Tariff third-country rate for heading 4402, charged on the customs (CIF) value
dutyLayers.legalStatus: Confirmed for UKGT 2026; verify the live line on the tariff tool each shipment.

vat.name: Import VAT
vat.recoverableNote: Standard rate, charged on the customs value plus duty plus freight/insurance and incidental UK port charges to the point of delivery. VAT-registered importers can use Postponed VAT Accounting (PVA) to account for import VAT on the VAT return rather than paying it at the border, then recover it as input tax under normal rules. The reduced 5% fuel-and-power rate does not apply: it is a UK domestic onward-supply rate for fuel held out solely for sale as fuel to a customer who will not resell it, so a wholesale import for resale is standard-rated regardless of quantity.

preference.note: Indonesia is eligible under the Developing Countries Trading Scheme (DCTS) "Standard Preferences" tier, but because the UKGT MFN rate for 4402 is already 0%, DCTS confers no additional duty benefit for this code. Charcoal is not among Indonesia's graduated chapters (oil-based products, footwear, musical instruments), so the 2026–2028 goods graduation does not affect it. Indonesia is scheduled to graduate out of the DCTS entirely from 1 January 2027 — but with MFN at 0%, the practical duty outcome is unchanged. No usable duty preference applies and none is needed.
preference.originDoc: DCTS origin declaration on the commercial invoice (the old GSP Form A is no longer accepted). Optional here because MFN duty is already 0%.

uk.dutyHistory: The UKGT MFN rate for heading 4402 has been 0% (free) for charcoal from most origins, and Indonesia's DCTS Standard-Preferences status adds no further benefit because the base is already free. The DCTS goods-graduation notice (1 Jan 2026 to 31 Dec 2028) does not list charcoal among Indonesia's graduated chapters, and Indonesia's full DCTS graduation from 1 January 2027 leaves the duty outcome unchanged at 0%.
uk.adcvd: No anti-dumping or countervailing-duty order was found for Chapter 44 charcoal of Indonesian origin; confirm with your customs broker.

entryNotes.ens: A Safety & Security declaration (ENS / S&S GB) is required for goods arriving into Great Britain. The carrier/operator is responsible for the S&S filing; it is not the exporter's task. The factory supplies the underlying cargo data.
entryNotes.declaration: The import declaration is filed through HMRC's Customs Declaration Service (CDS) by the buyer's customs broker, using a valid GB EORI number, the 4402 20 00 00 commodity code, and a direct-representation letter of authority. Most importers use Postponed VAT Accounting on the declaration.
entryNotes.gvms: Where the cargo moves through a port operating the Goods Vehicle Movement Service, a Goods Movement Reference (GMR) is generated to link the declaration to the physical movement. Container dwell time for UN 1361 Class 4.2 cargo is tightly limited, so the declaration must be ready on arrival to avoid demurrage.

items.label: UK REACH
items.value: UK REACH applies to substances and articles. No charcoal-specific UK REACH restriction was identified; rely on the safety data sheet (SDS) the factory provides. General product-safety rules apply at retail.

items.label: UK Timber Regulation (UKTR) / FLEGT
items.value: The OPSS UKTR enforcement report (2022–2025) lists the HS codes actually checked (4401, 4407, 4409, 4412) and does not list 4402 charcoal; the wood-fuel workstream covers firewood, logs, chips, pellets and briquettes (4401-type), not carbonised charcoal. Coconut-shell charcoal is an agricultural byproduct of Cocos nucifera, not timber, so it is not clearly in UKTR/FLEGT scope. Indonesia is the only country with a FLEGT VPA with the UK, so genuinely in-scope timber would need a FLEGT licence — confirm with OPSS that 4402 coconut-shell charcoal is out of scope before relying on the exemption.

items.label: Phytosanitary (APHA / BTOM)
items.value: Fully carbonised charcoal is generally outside the plant-health regulated-commodity scope, so no APHA phytosanitary certificate or IPAFFS pre-notification is normally required — confirm the commodity status via Defra's plant-health import-rules service, as this is subject to local Border Control Post interpretation of "wood products." Describe the goods on the invoice as fully carbonised, agglomerated charcoal to avoid a misclassification as a regulated wood product. Wood pallets and dunnage must be ISPM-15 compliant (heat-treated or fumigated and stamped).

htsCandidates.note: EU Combined Nomenclature / TARIC "Of shells or nuts" — the most specific subheading for carbonized coconut-shell charcoal. German ATLAS import declarations use the 11-digit code, the first 8 of which are this CN. Base (erga omnes) duty Free.

Residual "Other". Some Indonesian exporters historically declared coconut-shell charcoal here, but in the EU it mis-signals a generic wood-derived product and creates an ATLAS documentary-mismatch risk; General Rule of Interpretation 3(a) gives precedence to the more specific 4402.20. Same Free duty, so the rate does not change.

germany.htsNotes: Declare 4402 20 00 00 ("of shells or nuts") + UN 1361 Class 4.2 identically on every document — commercial invoice, packing list, bill of lading, and certificate of origin. Persistent use of the residual 4402.90 can trigger the German ATLAS risk-profiling algorithm and a manifest-correction fee even though the duty is identical. Do not declare as 3802 (activated carbon) or 4401 (fuel wood). Confirm the exact 11-digit ATLAS code with your customs broker.

dutyLayers.label: Customs duty (erga omnes / third-country MFN)
dutyLayers.basis: EU Common Customs Tariff for heading 4402, all origins; assessed on the customs (CIF) value
dutyLayers.legalStatus: Confirmed. MFN is already 0%, so Indonesia's loss of GSP preference does not raise the duty.

vat.name: Einfuhrumsatzsteuer (German import VAT)
vat.recoverableNote: Charged on the customs value plus duty plus domestic charges to the first destination. Charcoal does not qualify for the reduced 7% rate. Fully recoverable as input tax by a VAT-registered importer; import-VAT deferment (Aufschubkonto) and input-VAT deduction are available, recovered via the German VAT return.

preference.note: No usable duty benefit. EU MFN for 4402 is already 0%, so preference status is immaterial to duty. Indonesia's wood/wooden-articles section (S9a) graduated out of EU GSP on 1 January 2020, GSP preferences for the section were further suspended for 2026–2028, and Indonesia loses GSP beneficiary status entirely from 2027. Practically, this shifts the ATLAS entry from a preferential regime to a standard MFN regime but does not increase the tax burden.
preference.originDoc: Standard certificate of origin for trade statistics and surveillance; a REX origin statement would only matter if a preference were claimed (none avails here, so it is optional for duty).

germany.dutyHistory: Before 2026, importers could rely on a Form A or REX statement to claim GSP duty-free access for Indonesian charcoal; from 2026 that preference is suspended (Commission Implementing Regulation (EU) 2025/1909) and Indonesia loses GSP status from 2027. Because the underlying MFN rate is 0%, the change alters the declaration method, not the amount of duty paid.
germany.adcvd: No anti-dumping or countervailing-duty measure was found for heading 4402 charcoal from Indonesia in the EU's trade-defence records; confirm with your customs broker.

entryNotes.ics2: A pre-arrival Entry Summary Declaration (ENS) is mandatory under ICS2 Release 3, which is live for maritime cargo entering the EU. The vessel-operating carrier files the master-level ENS and the forwarder/NVOCC files the house-level ENS (plain-language goods description, HS6 minimum, consignee EORI). "No MRN, no load" is enforced; ICS1 was fully phased out on 1 September 2025. The filing is the carrier's and forwarder's responsibility — the Indonesian factory supplies the export data they need.
entryNotes.atlas: The import declaration is filed through the German ATLAS system by the buyer's customs broker under a customs power of attorney, using the 11-digit commodity code and the customs (CIF) value. The buyer needs a valid EU/German EORI; clearance cannot legally proceed without it. German terminals grant Class 4.2 cargo very short free time, so the broker should have the ATLAS entry ready against the arrival notice to avoid demurrage.

items.label: EUDR — scope
items.value: Heading 4402 wood charcoal (including shell or nut charcoal) is listed in Annex I of the EU Deforestation Regulation (Reg. (EU) 2023/1115), with no carve-out for coconut-shell charcoal, so EUDR applies.

items.label: EUDR — when it applies
items.value: Reg. (EU) 2025/2650 postponed application to 30 December 2026 for large and medium operators and 30 June 2027 for micro and small operators.

items.label: EUDR — who files
items.value: The EU importer (the first operator placing the product on the EU market) must submit a Due Diligence Statement (DDS) via the EU Information System (TRACES), demonstrating plot geolocation, deforestation-free status (land not deforested after 31 December 2020) and legality. The factory is working toward supplying the geolocation, legality and traceability evidence the buyer needs to file that DDS.

items.label: EU REACH + CLP
items.value: Charcoal is a registrable substance under EU REACH (not an SDS-only product): an EU importer placing more than one tonne per year on the market must either hold its own registration or be covered by the non-EU manufacturer's appointed Only Representative (OR) holding a Letter of Access to the existing joint registration, whose lead registrant is COALSTER GmbH. Two substance identities are in play — generic "Charcoal" (EC 240-383-3, CAS 16291-96-6) and the coconut-shell-specific "Charcoal coconut shell" (EC 271-974-4, CAS 68647-86-9); confirm with the lead registrant / OR which one your tapioca-bound composition registers under. CLP self-heating-substance classification and labelling apply (UN 1361, Class 4.2).

items.label: EUTR (predecessor)
items.value: The EU Timber Regulation continues transitionally for certain timber harvested before 29 June 2023 but is superseded by EUDR; coconut shell is an agricultural byproduct rather than timber.

htsCandidates.note: HS6 "Of shells or nuts" — the correct, most specific heading for coconut-shell charcoal under WCO GRI 3(a); GCC Common External Tariff duty 5% on CIF.

Full 12-digit GCC Integrated Customs Tariff extension (effective 1 Jan 2025) commonly cited for this product — UNVERIFIED against the live ZATCA tariff tool; confirm the exact 12-digit line with your broker before filing.

Residual "Other" — WRONG for coconut-shell charcoal. Declaring 4402.90 while the broker files 4402.20 creates a FASAH document mismatch that routes the dossier to the Red Lane for inspection. Do not use.

saudiArabia.htsNotes: Declare HS6 4402.20 uniformly on the commercial invoice, packing list, certificate of origin and bill of lading. The 12-digit national extension is UNVERIFIED here — confirm the exact line on ZATCA's Integrated Customs Tariff tool with your licensed Saudi customs broker; a code mismatch between origin documents and the FASAH declaration is the single most common cause of Red-Lane inspection, demurrage and penalties.

dutyLayers.label: Customs duty (GCC Common External Tariff)
dutyLayers.basis: Ad valorem on CIF value; GCC CET for goods from outside the GCC
dutyLayers.legalStatus: Confirmed baseline (GCC CET 2025). Final rate is subject to a real-time 12-digit ZATCA tariff lookup at entry; ZATCA amended select lines to 10–20% in late 2025/early 2026, but charcoal is not among the protected categories. Verify the line item at booking.

dutyLayers.label: ZATCA import service fee
dutyLayers.basis: Ad valorem on CIF value; minimum SAR 15, maximum SAR 500 per declaration
dutyLayers.legalStatus: Confirmed. Introduced October 2024, replacing the prior flat X-ray, information-exchange and processing fees.

vat.name: Value Added Tax (ZATCA)
vat.recoverableNote: Levied on the fully landed value (CIF + customs duty + ZATCA fees + port/local charges), not just the invoice value. Recoverable as input VAT by a VAT-registered Saudi importer holding a valid Commercial Registration and VAT certificate.

preference.note: No GCC or Saudi tariff preference exists for goods originating in Indonesia — GCC preferences are intra-GCC and specific-FTA only. The full 5% GCC CET applies and no preference document reduces it. A chamber-attested certificate of origin is still required for clearance, to prove national origin, not to claim a lower duty.
preference.originDoc: Certificate of origin attested by the Indonesian Chamber of Commerce (KADIN) / Ministry of Trade

saudiArabia.dutyHistory: Saudi Arabia moved from an 8-digit to a 12-digit GCC Integrated Customs Tariff on 1 January 2025, expanding the schedule from roughly 7,800 to over 13,400 lines; legacy 6- and 8-digit codes are now rejected by FASAH. ZATCA amended select tariff lines to WTO-bound ceilings of 10–20% in late 2025/early 2026 for industrial-protection categories, but charcoal under 4402 is not among them and the 5% baseline continues to apply.
saudiArabia.adcvd: No anti-dumping or countervailing-duty order was found for Indonesian charcoal under heading 4402 entering Saudi Arabia or the GCC; confirm with your customs broker.

entryNotes.fasah: Customs clearance runs through FASAH, the national single-window platform. The Saudi importer authorizes a licensed customs broker on FASAH, who files the customs declaration (Bayan) electronically. Duties, VAT and the ZATCA service fee are assessed and paid pre-clearance; the importer must hold a valid Commercial Registration and VAT certificate.
entryNotes.saber: A SABER Shipment Certificate of Conformity (SCoC) is mandatory and must exist in the system before the FASAH declaration is filed. SABER is auto-linked to FASAH by API; ZATCA no longer accepts post-arrival SCoCs or manual letters of undertaking, so a missing SCoC means the cargo is refused or forced to re-export. Secure the SCoC before the vessel departs Semarang.

items.label: SABER / SASO
items.value: Every shipment requires a SABER Shipment Certificate of Conformity (SCoC) to clear customs; shipments without one are refused or re-exported. The Saudi importer registers the product and obtains the certificate on the SABER platform.

items.label: Regulated vs non-regulated
items.value: Commercial SABER databases treat coconut-shell charcoal as a non-regulated product, needing only a Self-Declaration of Conformity plus the per-shipment SCoC rather than a third-party Product CoC under a named SASO Technical Regulation. Whether HS 4402.20 is classed regulated or non-regulated in SABER is UNVERIFIED for this code — confirm in SABER with an accredited conformity body before shipping.

items.label: SFDA
items.value: Shisha charcoal is a heat source, not tobacco, so it falls outside SFDA jurisdiction under the Anti-Smoking Law, which covers tobacco and waterpipe tobacco molasses (mu'assel). No SFDA tobacco registration applies to the charcoal.

items.label: Excise / selective tax
items.value: Saudi selective (excise) tax applies to tobacco, soft drinks and energy/sweetened drinks — not charcoal. No excise is levied on coconut-shell charcoal.

items.label: Arabic labelling
items.value: Arabic-language labelling requirements apply to goods placed on the Saudi market; confirm the exact label content with your importer and conformity body.

htsCandidates.note: ТН ВЭД ЕАЭС "Of shells or nuts" (из скорлупы или орехов) — the precise subheading for coconut-shell charcoal; carries the 5% EAEU base duty. Confirmed as the accepted line against filed Russian import declarations (IFCG records) for agglomerated coconut hookah-charcoal briquettes; declare here rather than the residual 4402 90 000 0.

Residual "Other" (прочий). Some Russian brokers declare tapioca-bound shisha briquettes here in practice (5% base duty confirmed on this line); General Rule of Interpretation 3(a) still favours the more specific 4402 20 "of shells or nuts". Confirm the line item with your broker before filing.

russia.htsNotes: Both 4402 subheadings carry the same 5% EAEU base duty, so the choice does not change the rate — but it does change the audit trail. Declare the botanical origin (Cocos nucifera) and the tapioca-starch binder (max 5%, no chemical accelerants) on the invoice: the Federal Customs Service (FTS) runs an automated risk system that can reclassify ambiguous or chemically adulterated charcoal into Chapter 38 (chemical products) or flag it as bamboo (4402 10), triggering sampling, delay, and misdeclaration penalties. Confirm classification with your Russian customs broker.

dutyLayers.label: Import customs duty (EAEU CCT, MFN)
dutyLayers.basis: Common Customs Tariff of the EAEU (ЕТТ), applied to the customs value (CIF to the Russian border)
dutyLayers.legalStatus: Confirmed for 4402 90; re-confirm the 4402 20 line item on alta.ru / the FCS tariff each shipment. Indonesia earns no preference (removed from the EAEU beneficiary list on 12 October 2021).

dutyLayers.label: Customs clearance fee (таможенный сбор)
dutyLayers.basis: Federal fee for customs operations, scaled by declared customs value, per Government Resolution No. 1638 of 23 Oct 2025 (effective 1 Jan 2026). Import brackets: ≤200,000 RUB = 1,231 RUB; 200,000–450,000 = 2,462; 450,000–1,200,000 = 4,924; 1,200,000–2,700,000 = 13,541; top bracket >10,000,000 = 73,860 (upper threshold raised from 7M to 10M RUB).
dutyLayers.legalStatus: Confirmed per Government Resolution No. 1638 of 23 Oct 2025; the intermediate 2.7M–10M RUB brackets are not itemised here — confirm the exact bracket for your declared value with your broker.

vat.name: Import VAT (НДС)
vat.recoverableNote: Raised from 20% to 22% effective 1 January 2026 by Federal Law No. 425-FZ of 28 Nov 2025. Charged on the customs value plus duty. Registered Russian VAT payers can deduct import VAT (Arts. 171–172 of the Tax Code, within three years, against the customs declaration and proof of payment). Charcoal does not qualify for the reduced 10% rate.

preference.note: No usable duty preference. Indonesia was removed from the EAEU unified system of tariff preferences (developing-country beneficiary list) effective 12 October 2021, so the full 5% MFN duty applies. The Indonesia–EAEU Free Trade Agreement was signed in December 2025 and promises 0% on most lines including charcoal, but it remains pending parliamentary ratification across the member states — the 0% rate is NOT yet in force, so plan on the 5% baseline until the Eurasian Economic Commission publishes the entry-into-force date.
preference.originDoc: Certificate of Origin per shipment; GSP Form A does not reduce duty today, and the future Indonesia–EAEU FTA preference will require the FTA's own origin certificate, not Form A

russia.dutyHistory: Indonesia previously received the EAEU developing-country preference (a 25% reduction, i.e. 75% of the base rate) but was removed from the beneficiary list on 12 October 2021 (EEC Council Decision No. 17 of 5 March 2021), which cut the list from 103 to 29 countries — so the full 5% MFN rate has applied since. The signed-but-unratified Indonesia–EAEU FTA (December 2025) is the next possible change; until it enters into force, the rate stays at 5%.
russia.adcvd: No anti-dumping or countervailing-duty measure was found for Chapter 44 charcoal from Indonesia in the EAEU/Russian databases; confirm with your customs broker.

entryNotes.declaration: An electronic customs declaration (ED-2 / ДТ) is filed with the Federal Customs Service (ФТС / FCS) under the EAEU Customs Code by the Russian importer or their licensed customs broker. The filing requires a registered foreign-trade contract (with a bank-issued Unique Contract Number / UIN where the contract value exceeds 3 million RUB for currency control), a customs broker power of attorney, and notarized Russian translations of the foreign documents (commercial invoice, MSDS, certificates). Customs value is assessed on a CIF basis to the Russian border; an under-statistical declared value can trigger a valuation query and a security deposit, so keep a full audit trail (export declaration, wire confirmation, freight invoice).

items.label: EAC certification
items.value: Charcoal (including coconut-shell charcoal fuel) is generally NOT subject to mandatory EAC certification or declaration under EAEU technical regulations. Importers typically obtain a refusal/negative letter (отказное письмо) from an accredited body confirming no certificate is required, which customs expects at clearance.

items.label: GOST
items.value: GOST 7657-84 ("Уголь древесный. Технические условия") exists for wood charcoal but compliance is voluntary, not a mandatory import gate.

items.label: TR TS 021/2011
items.value: Some Russian certification agencies argue a food-safety declaration under TR TS 021/2011 may apply IF charcoal is sold for food/grill cooking — shisha charcoal is a heat-source fuel, so this is generally not applicable. The commercial sources conflict; UNVERIFIED — confirm with an accredited certification body.

items.label: Phytosanitary control
items.value: Rosselkhoznadzor treats wood/shell charcoal as a plant-origin commodity subject to phytosanitary control: a Phytosanitary Certificate from the Indonesian NPPO is required, with pre-arrival notification and possible physical sampling at the border (importer's cost).

items.label: Carrier acceptance
items.value: Major Western container lines (Maersk, MSC, CMA CGM, Hapag-Lloyd, ONE) suspended new Russia bookings effective 1 March 2022. Cargo now moves via regional, neutral and Asian carriers through transshipment hubs; Vladivostok (RUVVO) is the most practical routing for Indonesian-origin cargo. DG Class 4.2 acceptance to Russian ports is carrier-specific and UNVERIFIED — confirm a DG-accepting carrier before committing.

items.label: Banking & payment
items.value: Major Russian banks have been cut from SWIFT since March 2022; payment requires non-sanctioned banks, local-currency settlement (RUB / IDR / CNY), intermediary banks (China / UAE / Central Asia) or alternative messaging (SPFS / CIPS). The EU 20th sanctions package (in force 24 April 2026) added an Indonesian port (Karimun Oil Terminal) to its restricted list, signalling sanctions reach into Indonesia–Russia logistics — confirm a sanctions-clear payment channel before quoting.

specs.additives: none — no chemical additives or igniting agents

tiers.note: Cleanest burn — minimal tray residue and the best airflow; the premium tier for shisha use.
A clean, reliable burn for everyday shisha service.
Higher residue than the upper tiers; a value option still within common quality bounds.

testingVideo.name: Mulia Charcoal burn and ash test
testingVideo.description: A short demonstration of a Mulia Charcoal coal through a full burn — lighting, the working session, and the silver-white, low-residue ash it leaves.

factoryTourVideo.name: Tour of Mulia Charcoal factory in Semarang, Indonesia (3 minutes)
factoryTourVideo.description: Three-minute walkthrough of the Mulia Charcoal coconut shell briquetting factory in Semarang, Central Java — carbonization ovens, briquetting lines, drying tunnel, QC station and packing.
